---
title: "analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# reading in tidied data
read_csv("sat_data.csv")
```

Steph's Section:

```{r}
# Analyzing SAT scores in a histogram

ggplot(data = sat_data, aes(x = SAT.Total)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(
    title = "Distrubution of SAT Scores Across NYC High Schools,\n2011 - 2012",
    x = "SAT Score",
    y = "# of Schools"
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(sat_data$SAT.Total, na.rm = TRUE), color = "red") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
# Analyzing SAT Scores in a summary table

library(gt)
summary_table <- tibble(
  Metric = c("Lowest SAT Score", "Highest SAT Score"),
  
  Score = c(
    min(sat_data$SAT.Total, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(sat_data$SAT.Total, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  
  School = c(
    sat_data$Name[which.min(sat_data$SAT.Total)],
    sat_data$Name[which.max(sat_data$SAT.Total)]
  ),
  
  Test_Takers = c(
    sat_data$Num.of.SAT.Test.Takers[which.min(sat_data$SAT.Total)],
    sat_data$Num.of.SAT.Test.Takers[which.max(sat_data$SAT.Total)]
  )
)

summary_table$School[is.na(summary_table$School)] <- "—"

summary_table |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title = "NYC High School SAT Scores (2011–2012)"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    Metric = "Metric",
    Score = "SAT Score",
    School = "School Name",
    Test_Takers = "Number of Test Takers"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(
    columns = Score,
    decimals = 1
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(14)
  )
```
